comparisonMethod.canonicalScope: Five canonical active baseline files named by the Phase 1 instruction.
comparisonMethod.patchScope: Every regular file returned by rg --files under CP11_baseline_patch through CP15_baseline_patch.
comparisonMethod.proofRule: A patch effect is treated as materialized only when the patch text is directly present, structurally represented by a cited current canonical heading or section, or explicitly superseded by a cited current canonical section.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ### CP11-C.3 Hard constraints and optimisation objectives
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: Report-only posture is not a constraint strength. A report-only sustainability indicator, metric posture, objective note, or priority annotation may require disclosure, monitoring, evidence request, or review, but it must not be represented as a `SustainabilityConstraint` or used as a hard-constraint pass/fail condition unless separate active charter law classifies it as a hard constraint.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: PHASE1-CP11-001 was resolved in Phase 1B by approved materialization of accepted CP11 patch text under CP11-C.3. This changed active authority content but did not introduce new semantic law; it made accepted CP11 hardening text explicit in the canonical Constitution.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP11 Sustainable Autonomous Farming Charter alignment addendum - 2026-05-21
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP11 introduces the following baseline-recognised charter-governance concepts.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: CP11 concept rows including SustainableFarmingCharter, CharterApplicabilityContext, SustainabilityConstraint, ObjectivePriority, SustainabilityEvidenceRequirement, CharterException, CharterBreach, RiskBudget, and RegretBudget are present in the canonical alignment register.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP11 Sustainable Autonomous Farming Charter hostile-review addendum - 2026-05-21
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: A hostile reader should treat CP11 as a bounded constitutional extension that makes sustainability governable, not as proof that OFARM can autonomously run sustainable farming operations.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The canonical hostile review includes CP11 closed gaps, still-open risks, and the no-autonomous-sustainability or cyber-physical-execution verdict.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP11 Sustainable Autonomous Farming Charter readiness and claim-limit addendum - 2026-05-21
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP11 updates readiness posture by adding a bounded Sustainable Autonomous Farming Charter governance layer.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The canonical readiness memo includes CP11 allowed claims, blocked claims, and evidence required for stronger CP11 claims.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP12 Cyber-Physical Mission Envelope baseline addendum - 2026-05-28
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: Physical mission authority is not produced by recommendation, plan, preflight success, CP11 charter pass, agent confidence, tool invocation success, machine capability declaration, command acknowledgement, telemetry receipt, or adapter output alone.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP12 baseline, runtime, alignment, readiness, and hostile-review addendum headings and distinctive non-claim text are present in the five canonical files.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM Alignment Register v0.13 - CP12 update candidate
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: The following concepts are OFARM-owned or OFARM-governed CP12 concepts.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP12 alignment update heading, concept rows, and residual-debt posture are directly present in the canonical alignment register.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM final hostile review after gap closure - CP12 update candidate
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP12 is the correct next bounded amendment after CP11 if, and only if, it remains mission-envelope law and does not become a robotics product specification, vendor protocol, or production autonomy claim.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP12 hostile review update is directly represented in the canonical hostile review.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM post-gap-closure readiness memo - CP12 addendum candidate
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP12 does **not** make OFARM production robot-ready or machine-control-ready.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP12 readiness posture, allowed claims, blocked claims, and explicit deferrals are directly represented in the canonical readiness memo.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP13 Learning, Experimentation, and Farm Memory baseline addendum - 2026-05-29
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: Mission or operation records may inform learning, but they do not become learning law by themselves.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP13 baseline, runtime, alignment, readiness, and hostile-review addendum headings and distinctive boundary text are represented in the five canonical files.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP13 Learning, Experimentation, and Farm Memory alignment addendum - 2026-05-29
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP13 concepts are OFARM-owned because they govern learning, experimentation, causal evidence, farm memory, promotion decisions, and output qualification.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP13 alignment rows and ownership note are present in the canonical alignment register.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP13 Learning, Experimentation, and Farm Memory hostile-review addendum - 2026-05-29
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP13 closes a conceptual gap around learning and farm memory but remains implementation-directed with bounded debt.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP13 hostile-review update text is present in the canonical hostile review.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP13 Learning, Experimentation, and Farm Memory readiness and claim-limit addendum - 2026-05-29
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP13 machine contracts remain draft/non-default until separate currentness promotion.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP13 readiness addendum is present in the canonical readiness memo.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP14 Farm-to-Farm Intelligence Boundary baseline addendum - 2026-05-29
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP14 introduces a bounded farm-to-farm intelligence boundary layer into OFARM model law.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP14 baseline, runtime, alignment, readiness, and hostile-review addendum effects are represented in the five canonical files.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM Alignment Register v0.13 - CP14 Update
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: Add the CP14 concept family as OFARM-owned or OFARM-governed cross-farm intelligence boundary concepts.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP14 alignment update headings, concept family, and non-promotion language are present in the canonical alignment register.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM Final Hostile Review - CP14 Update
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: A hostile reader should treat CP14 as a necessary farm-to-farm intelligence boundary extension, not as evidence that OFARM is now a production farm-to-farm intelligence system.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP14 hostile-review update is directly represented in the canonical hostile review.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM Readiness Gate Memo - CP14 Addendum
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP14 adds a governed farm-to-farm intelligence boundary. It is a model/runtime governance closure, not production farm-to-farm intelligence readiness.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP14 readiness addendum is directly represented in the canonical readiness memo.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: ## CP15 Agentic Software Delivery and Model Deployment Governance baseline addendum - 2026-05-30
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: Deployment requires explicit delivery-envelope governance, authority trace, provenance/SBOM/security/conformance evidence, applicable CP11/CP12/CP13/CP14 gates, runtime-surface binding, deployment authorization, rollback posture, output qualification, and conformance evidence.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP15 baseline, runtime, alignment, readiness, and hostile-review addendum effects are represented in the five canonical files.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM Alignment Register v0.13 - CP15 update
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP15 does not promote CP11, CP12, CP13, CP14, or CP15 draft/non-default machine contracts to current/default.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP15 concept additions and non-promotion rules are directly represented in the canonical alignment register.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM hostile review - CP15 update
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP15 closes a major conceptual gap: generated software, generated adapters, generated mappings, prompt/workflow changes, model candidates, and deployment attempts are now governed artifacts rather than hidden authority.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP15 hostile-review update is directly represented in the canonical hostile review.

evidence.matchedHeadingOrAnchor: # OFARM readiness memo - CP15 addendum
evidence.matchedTextSnippet: CP15 adds model-law and runtime-boundary support for agentic software delivery and model deployment governance.
evidence.semanticMatchNote: The CP15 readiness addendum is directly represented in the canonical readiness memo.

resolvedDebt.description: The accepted CP11 rule that report-only posture is not a constraint strength is now explicitly materialized under CP11-C.3 in the canonical Constitution.

CP11.reason: All CP11 patch files are represented in the canonical baseline, runtime architecture, alignment register, readiness memo, or hostile review after Phase 1B approved materialization.

CP12.reason: All CP12 patch files are represented in the canonical baseline, runtime architecture, alignment register, readiness memo, or hostile review.

CP13.reason: All CP13 patch files are represented in the canonical baseline, runtime architecture, alignment register, readiness memo, or hostile review.

CP14.reason: All CP14 patch files are represented in the canonical baseline, runtime architecture, alignment register, readiness memo, or hostile review.

CP15.reason: All CP15 patch files are represented in the canonical baseline, runtime architecture, alignment register, readiness memo, or hostile review.

phaseGate.reason: All CP11 through CP15 baseline patch folders are now materialized in canonical baseline files or supporting canonical active-baseline files. No semantic-equivalence blocker remains.

phase0DebtUpdates.description: Semantic equivalence is proven for CP11 through CP15 baseline patch folders after Phase 1B approved materialization of accepted CP11 hardening text.

phase_1b_materialization.targetHeading: ### CP11-C.3 Hard constraints and optimisation objectives
phase_1b_materialization.insertedText: Report-only posture is not a constraint strength. A report-only sustainability indicator, metric posture, objective note, or priority annotation may require disclosure, monitoring, evidence request, or review, but it must not be represented as a `SustainabilityConstraint` or used as a hard-constraint pass/fail condition unless separate active charter law classifies it as a hard constraint. Report-only posture belongs to objective, metric, or priority surfaces and remains subordinate to hard constraints, evidence sufficiency, claim-basis, authority, and output-qualification gates.

validationRun.details: Pre-Phase 1 status showed only Phase 0 clean-baseline ledger files: CLEAN_BASELINE_COMPLETION_LEDGER.json and CLEAN_BASELINE_CURRENTNESS_STRING_AUDIT.json.

validationRun.command: python3 -c "import json; [json.load(open(p)) for p in ['CLEAN_BASELINE_CURRENTNESS_STRING_AUDIT.json', 'CLEAN_BASELINE_COMPLETION_LEDGER.json', 'CLEAN_BASELINE_SEMANTIC_EQUIVALENCE_LEDGER.json']]; print('json parse ok')"
validationRun.details: JSON parse check is non-mutating and permitted in Phase 1.
Expected result is only clean-baseline ledger files created or updated through Phase 1.

validationSkipped.reason: The full suite is required later, but skipped in Phase 1 because it can invoke conformance runners that write tracked result files. Phase 1 validation is limited to non-mutating status, hash, reference-scan, and JSON parse checks.

acceptanceCriteria.description: All five canonical active baseline files are listed with sha256 hashes.
Every file under CP11_baseline_patch through CP15_baseline_patch is listed or explicitly excluded with reason.
Every CP patch file maps to one canonical active baseline file or records unresolved debt.
No CP patch folder is marked lineage-only unless all its active effects are MATERIALIZED_IN_CANONICAL_BASELINE or DUPLICATE_EVIDENCE.
Any PARTIALLY_MATERIALIZED, NOT_MATERIALIZED, or NEEDS_REVIEW item is recorded as unresolved debt.
No canonical active authority file content is modified.
No current/ reader-view folders are created.
Phase 0 debts remain open unless Phase 1 has direct evidence to update only the semantic-equivalence debt.
git status after Phase 1 shows only the new or updated clean-baseline ledger files expected for Phase 1.
